no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what's broken (crash on non-string) and what the fix should achieve (throw TypeError with specific message), but does not specify the exact file to change (BunPlugin.cpp), the exact function (JSMock__jsModuleMock), or the exact code changes (isString() guard). A developer must still locate and reason about the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests verify behavior (TypeError thrown with the right message for non-string args, existing tests still pass) rather than implementation details. An alternative fix using e.g. jsTypeOf check, isObject/isNumber guards, or a different C++ approach would all pass as long as the TypeError with the specified message is thrown. The test discovery uses broad keyword matching (TypeError, SharedArrayBuffer, etc.) so different test file names/structures would be found.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests execute the agent's test file via 'bun test' subprocess and check the return code. The pass-to-pass tests also execute actual test suites and commands (bun test, tsc, oxlint). They verify runtime behavior, not just text content of files.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests run the agent's test file which must assert that mock.module(SharedArrayBuffer, ...) throws TypeError instead of crashing. Without the actual fix in the C++ code, the process would crash (segfault) rather than throw, so the test would fail. A stub/no-op implementation would not satisfy these tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires: (1) TypeError for non-string args - tested via agent test execution, (2) exact error message - the agent test is expected to assert on this, (3) existing argumentCount/isEmpty guards preserved - tested by test_existing_guards_preserved. All three requirements are covered.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names like test_type_guard_before_tostring, test_error_message_descriptive, test_existing_guards_preserved, and include docstrings explaining what each test verifies. They are grouped into fail-to-pass and pass-to-pass sections with clear comments.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction does not name the buggy file (BunPlugin.cpp), the specific function (JSMock__jsModuleMock), or the line number. It only describes the observable bug and expected behavior, leaving localization to the agent.
